title: Rahul vs The State Of Madhya Pradesh on 20 September, 2019
doc:
Rahul vs The State Of Madhya Pradesh on 20 September, 2019

Author: Sunil Kumar Awasthi

Bench: Sunil Kumar Awasthi

                   M.Cr.C. No.39171/2019                                                   1




                            THE HIGH COURT OF MADHYA PRADESH
                                                 M.Cr.C. No.39171/2019
                                        (Rahul Jain vs. The State of Madhya Pradesh)

                   Indore, Dated: 20/09/2019
                                  Shri Sunil Jain, learned Senior Counsel with Shri Bharat
                   Sharma, learned counsel for the applicant.
                                  Shri Yogesh Gupta, learned Public Prosecutor for the
                   respondent/State.

Heard, Case-diary perused.

This is a repeat (seventh) application under Section 439, Cr.P.C for grant of bail in connection with Crime No.1139/2015, registered at police station-Lasudia, District-Indore, concerning offence under Sections 420, 467, 468/34 of IPC.

The first and fourth application moved on behalf of the applicant were dismissed as withdrawn vide orders dated 01/12/2017 and 12/11/2018 passed in M.Cr.C. Nos.22236/2017 and 43632/2018, respectively. The Second, third, fifth and sixth applications were dismissed vide orders dated 23/03/2018, 05/07/2018, 02/01/2019 and 01/03/2019 passed in M.Cr.C. Nos.5796/2018, 19479/2018, 49065/2018 and 6702/2019, respectively.

As per prosecution story, a written complaint was filed by the complainant Divya Jain alleging that the present applicant who was working as a placement officer in RKDF college, Indore has assured to the complainant and others for their guaranteed placement in multinational companies through campus selection. It is also alleged that after getting selection of 40-50 candidates, the candidates had deposited about Rs.40.00 lakhs in the bank account of the applicant and the other accused persons. After some time, the Digitally signed by Sumati Jagadeesan Date: 23/09/2019 10:53:31 M.Cr.C. No.39171/2019 2 complainant and the selected candidates came to know that the whole process of the campus selection is forged and intentionally done by the applicant. On that basis police registered the aforesaid offence against the present applicant.

Learned Senior counsel for the applicant has submitted that applicant is innocent and he has been falsely implicated in the present crime. He is in custody since 02/11/2017. Investigation is over and charge-sheet has been filed. Prosecution has already examined three witnesses before the trial Court. Complainant-Divya Jain has accepted that applicant has organised campus selection in RKDF college and they selected students for employment, therefore, it cannot be said that the applicant has committed any forgery on the name of campus selection. The complainant also admitted in the cross-examination that she has repaid Rs.8,40,000/- which was received from the students for securing employment. None of the students has made any complaint against the applicant. Earlier application of the applicant was dismissed on the ground that other students have also made allegation against the applicant that he has received amount from them and they have not got any job, however, after that the remaining students Mamta Kushwah and Vishesh Gupta have also been examined before the trial Court and they have not supported the prosecution story and turned hostile. Conclusion of trial will take sufficient long time. Under these changed circumstances, learned counsel prays for grant of bail to the applicant.

Learned Public Prosecutor opposes the prayer by contending that earlier application of the applicant has already been rejected on merit and looking to the crime committed by the applicant he not entitled for grant of bail, hence he prayed for dismissal of the Digitally signed by Sumati Jagadeesan Date: 23/09/2019 10:53:31 M.Cr.C. No.39171/2019 3 same.

After considering the arguments advanced by learned counsel for the parties and looking to the fact that the complainant and other witnesses, who have made allegations against the applicant that they have paid the amount to the applicant, but they did not get the job, have been examined before the trial Court, they have not supported the prosecution case and turned hostile. Under these changed circumstances I am inclined to grant bail to the applicant.

Accordingly, the application filed by the applicant is allowed. The applicant is directed to be released on bail on his/her furnishing a personal bond in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/- (Rupees One lakh only), with two solvent sureties each in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand only) to the satisfaction of trial Court, with a condition that he will deposit the 50% of the amount which has been repaid by the complainant to the students and that he/she shall remain present before the Court concerned during trial and shall also abide by the conditions enumerated under Section 437 (3) of Cr.P.C.

This order shall be effective till the end of the trial, however, in case of bail jump, it shall become ineffective.

Certified copy as per rules.

(S. K. AWASTHI) JUDGE sumathi Digitally signed by Sumati Jagadeesan Date: 23/09/2019 10:53:31
